revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:أسطورة الخلق اليابانية]][[da:Skabelsesmyten i Shinto]][[en:Japanese creation myth]][[es:Mito japonés de la Creación]][[fa:افسانه آفرینش ژاپنی]][[hu:Japán teremtéstörténet]][[ja:天地開闢 (日本神話)]][[ko:천지개벽 (일본 신화)]][[ru:Японский миф о сотворении мира]][[tl:Mito ng paglikhang Hapones]][[zh:开天辟地 (日本神话)]]
{{One source|date=May 2024}}{{short description|Japanese mythology about the creation of the world and of Japan}} [[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-en.svg|thumb|300px|Table illustrating the [[kami]] that appeared during the creation of Heaven and Earth according to [[Japanese mythology]].]] In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{nihongo|'''Japanese Creation Myth'''|天地開闢|Tenchi-kaibyaku|extra=Literally "Creation of Heaven & Earth"}} is the {{ill|Creation myth|en|Creation myth|qid=Q215304|lt=story|1=Creation myth}} that describes the legendary birth of the celestial and creative world, the birth of the first gods, and the birth of the [[Japanese archipelago]].
This story is described at the beginning of the [[Kojiki]], the first book written in Japan (712), and in the [[Nihon Shoki]] (720). Both form the literary basis of Japanese mythology and [[Shinto]]; however, the story differs in some aspects between these works.{{Citation needed|date=December 2015}}
==Myth== At the beginning the universe was immersed in a beaten and shapeless kind of matter (chaos), sunk in silence. Later there were sounds indicating the movement of particles. With this movement, the light and the lightest particles rose but the particles were not as fast as the light and could not go higher. Thus, the light was at the top of the Universe, and below it, the particles formed first the clouds and then Heaven, which was to be called {{nihongo |''[[Takamagahara]]''|高天原|extra="High Plain of Heaven"}}. The rest of the particles that had not risen formed a huge mass, dense and dark, to be called Earth.<ref name="kojiki-p67-70">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=67–70}}</ref>
When Takamagahara was formed, the first three gods appeared:<ref name="kojiki-p71">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=71}}</ref>
*'''{{nihongo|[[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]]|天之御中主神, Ame-no-Minakanushi}}''' *'''{{nihongo|[[Takamimusubi]]|高御産巣日神, Taka-mi-musuhi-no-kami}} and''' *'''{{nihongo|[[Kamimusubi]]|神産巣日神, Kami-musuhi-no-kami}}'''.
Then these gods:
*'''{{nihongo|[[Umashi-Ashikabi-Hikoji|Umashi-ashi-kabi-hikoji]]|宇摩志阿斯訶備比古遅神, Umashi-ashi-kabi-hikoji-no-kami}} and''' *'''{{nihongo|[[Ame-no-Tokotachi|Ame-no-toko-tachi]]|天之常立神, Ame-no-toko-tachi-no-kami}}'''
These five deities, known as [[Kotoamatsukami]], appeared spontaneously, did not have a definite sex, did not have partners (''{{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|qid=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami|1=Hitorigami}}'') and went into hiding after their emergence. These gods are not mentioned in the rest of the mythology.<ref name="kojiki-p71"/> ===Kamiyonanayo=== {{main|Kamiyonanayo}} [[File:Izanagi and Izanami giving birth to Japan c1870 after Kawanabe Kyosai.jpg|thumb|Izanagi and Izanami giving birth to Japan]] Then two other gods arose:<ref name="kojiki-p72">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=72}}</ref>
*'''{{nihongo|[[Kuni-no-Tokotachi]]|国之常立神, Kuni-no-tokotachi-no-kami}} and''' *'''{{nihongo|{{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumonu|lt=Toyo-kumono|ja|トヨクモノ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu|qq=}}|豊雲野神, Toyo-kumono-no-kami}}'''
These gods also emerged spontaneously, did not have a defined sex, did not have a partner, and hid at birth.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>
Then, five pairs of gods were born (for a total of ten deities), each pair consisting of a male deity and a female deity:<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>
* {{nihongo|{{ill|Uhijini and Suhijini|lt=Uhijini|ja|ウヒヂニ・スヒヂニ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uhijini and Suhijini|qq=}}|宇比地邇神}} and his younger sister (and wife) {{nihongo|{{ill|Uhijini and Suhijini|lt=Suhijini|ja|ウヒヂニ・スヒヂニ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uhijini and Suhijini|qq=}}|須比智邇神}}, * {{nihongo|[[Tsunugui and Ikugui|Tsunuguhi]]|角杙神}} and his younger sister (and wife) {{nihongo|[[Ikuguhi]]|活杙神}}, * {{nihongo|[[Ōtonoji]]|意富斗能地神}} and his younger sister (and wife) {{nihongo|[[Ōtonobe]]|大斗乃弁神}}, * {{nihongo|[[Omodaru]]|於母陀流神}} and his younger sister (and wife) {{nihongo|[[Aya-kashiko-ne]]|阿夜訶志古泥神}} and * {{nihongo|[[Izanagi]]|伊邪那岐神}} and his younger sister (and wife) {{nihongo|{{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}}|伊邪那美神}}
All deities from Kuni-no-koto-tachi to Izanami are collectively called {{nihongo|[[Kamiyonanayo]]|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|extra="Seven Divine Generations"}}.<ref name="kojiki-p72"/>
Following the creation of Heaven and Earth and the appearance of these primordial gods, Izanagi and Izanami went on to create the [[Japanese archipelago]] (''[[Kuniumi]]'') and gave birth to a large number of gods (''[[Kamiumi]]'').<ref name="kojiki-p73-86">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=73–86}}</ref>
==See also== *{{ill|Creation myth|en|Creation myth|qid=Q215304|lt=Creation myth|1=Creation myth}}
==References== {{reflist}}
===Bibliography=== *{{cite book|last=Chamberlain|first=Basil Hall|title=The Kojiki: Japanese Records of Ancient Matters|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=zVw392cJrJoC|access-date=9 February 20dd|year=2008|publisher=Forgotten Books|isbn=978-1-60506-938-8}} *{{cite web |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/2007dd10023248/http://www.clubmanga.net/files/cursos/historia/CH_Mitologia.pdf |title=Génesis del mundo y aparición de los primeros dioses|trans-title=Genesis of the world and appearance of the first gods|archive-date=2007-dd-10|language=es|format=PDF|url=http://www.clubmanga.net/files/cursos/historia/CH_Mitologia.pdf}}
{{Jmyth navbox long}}
